pattern: A min-heap seeded with the smallest pair expands frontier pairs in non-decreasing sum order, extracting exactly k pairs without exploring the full cross-product.
hints: The smallest pair always involves nums1[0] and nums2[0]; from any pair (i,j) the next candidates are (i,j+1) and (i+1,0) — these form a natural heap frontier.
Push (nums1[i]+nums2[j], i, j) into a min-heap; when you pop (i,j) push (i,j+1) if valid, and push (i+1,0) only when j==0 to avoid duplicate insertions.
Use a visited set keyed on (i,j) to prevent pushing the same index pair twice; extract k times from the heap and collect the value pairs.

edge_inputs.description: standard example, three smallest from two arrays
k larger than total pairs, return all
large negative plus large positive, verify sum ordering

stress.description: medium arrays with moderate k and some repeated values
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n1 = max(1, size // 2)
    n2 = max(1, size - n1)
    nums1 = sorted(rng.randrange(-100, 101) for _ in range(n1))
    nums2 = sorted(rng.randrange(-100, 101) for _ in range(n2))
    k = min(rng.randrange(1, 201), n1 * n2)
    return [nums1, nums2, k]
